Workflows where a more recent successor in the same family scores higher

public_view.plain_english:
NVIDIA's open vision-language-action model for humanoid robots —
GR00T N1 (March 2025), N1.5, N1.7. Released Apache 2.0 with the
full Isaac Lab + Isaac Sim ecosystem. The Cosmos Predict / Reason
/ Policy world models complement GR00T as simulation + planning
upstream of the action model.
public_view.analogy:
NVIDIA's robotics play is the same as their AI play — give away
the software (GR00T + Isaac), sell the hardware (GPUs + Jetson +
forthcoming robot accelerators).
public_view.investment_angle:
NVIDIA's robotics stack is cross-promoted with their AI stack —
every robotics startup that uses Isaac is also locked into
CUDA + Jetson. GR00T's open weights make the Isaac ecosystem the
default for academic and commercial robotics work.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Open VLA at the 2.2B parameter scale, integrated with the
industry-standard simulator (Isaac Sim) and the dominant
embedded compute platform (Jetson). NVIDIA is positioning to be
upstream of every robotics company the way they are for every AI
company.

nodes.body_md:
## NVIDIA's robotics stack

| Layer | Product |
|---|---|
| World model | Cosmos Predict 2.5 / Reason 2 / Policy |
| Foundation VLA | Isaac GR00T N1 / N1.5 / N1.7 |
| Simulation | Isaac Sim / Isaac Lab |
| Compute | Jetson + DGX |
| Datasets | RoboMIND 2.0 partners |

nodes.title: Gemini 2.5 Pro — Google's Native Reasoning Frontier

feels_like: Long context is the headline — 91.5% on 128k and 83.1% on 1M long-context retrieval, ahead of every other frontier model
Independent testers confirm reliable retrieval up to ~800K tokens; the last 200K degrades
Genuine 'put a whole codebase or book in the prompt' workflow — not theatre
AIME 2024 92.0% and GPQA Diamond 84.0% put it in the top tier on reasoning
Built-in thinking mode is a default, not a switch — you don't have to pick a 'reasoning model'
Pricing tiers up past 200K tokens (2x surcharge) — long-context isn't free
best_for: Whole-codebase analysis, refactors across many files at once
Long-form document QA (books, contracts, research papers)
Multi-doc RAG replacement when context budget is generous
Mixed reasoning + multimodal tasks (still strong on MMMU, MMLU-Pro)
not_ideal_for: Pure agentic-coding leaderboards — Claude Opus 4.5 and DeepSeek V4 lead SWE-bench
Cost-bound bulk inference past 200K tokens (price doubles)
Self-hosted / open-weights deployments

public_view.plain_english:
Google DeepMind's first model to integrate reasoning natively into
its main product line — earlier reasoning attempts (Gemini 2.0
Flash Thinking, December 2024) were experimental side products.
Gemini 2.5 Pro became the default Gemini for paying users in
March 2025 and held a meaningful lead on math reasoning
benchmarks (AIME, Humanity's Last Exam) for ~2 months.
public_view.analogy:
Google catching up — and briefly leading — on reasoning, after
spending most of 2024 in second or third place. The "Gemini was
always 6 months behind" pattern broke here.
public_view.investment_angle:
Gemini 2.5 Pro restored Google's competitive credibility after a
rough 2023-24. The 1M-token context (with 2M experimental)
paired with NotebookLM created the strongest "research
assistant" UX in the market — the use case where Google's
distribution moat (Workspace, Drive, Gmail) compounds. Google's
AI revenue line item in Cloud reportedly tripled YoY in 2025
largely on Gemini-driven workloads.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Gemini 2.5 Pro proved Google could ship at the frontier on
Google's calendar — not just react to OpenAI. The combination
with TPU v6 / v7 manufacturing capacity gave Google unique
cost-structure advantages that became visible in 2025-26 pricing.

citations.title: Gemini 2.5: Our most intelligent AI model

nodes.body_md:
## Gemini 2.5 Pro vs frontier peers (Q2 2025)

| Model | Date | Org | AIME | GPQA | SWE-bench V | Context |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| o1 | 2024-09 | OpenAI | 83.3% | 78.0% | — | 128k |
| Claude 3.7 Sonnet | 2025-02 | Anthropic | 80.0% | 84.8% | 70.3% | 200k |
| **Gemini 2.5 Pro** | **2025-03** | **Google DeepMind** | **92.0%** | **84.0%** | **63.8%** | **1M** |
| o3 | 2025-04 | OpenAI | 96.7% | 87.7% | 71.7% | 200k |
| Claude 4 Opus | 2025-05 | Anthropic | — | 83.3% | 79.4% | 200k |

Gemini 2.5 Pro briefly held the AIME lead (March-April 2025) before o3's full release reclaimed it. The 1M-context advantage combined with NotebookLM's research-assistant productization was the durable value.

## Lineage in the Gemini line

| Gen | Date | Headline |
|---|---|---|
| Gemini 1.0 | 2023-12 | tiered family launch |
| Gemini 1.5 Pro | 2024-02 | 1M-token context |
| Gemini 2.0 Flash | 2024-12 | reasoning + agentic upgrades |
| **Gemini 2.5 Pro** | **2025-03** | **native thinking integration** |
| Gemini 3 | 2025-Q4 | next major reset (rumored) |

The shift from "we have reasoning as an experimental feature" to "reasoning is a parameter on the main model" was the productization move that mattered, not the benchmark numbers.

nodes.title: Midjourney V7 — Aesthetic Frontier

feels_like: Complete rebuild from scratch — not an incremental update — released April 3, 2025; default June 17
Anatomical accuracy jumped 40% (hands, faces) — Midjourney's longest-running weakness finally meaningfully addressed
Prompt understanding 35% better — simpler prompts now produce closer-to-intent results
Text rendering remains the Achilles heel — only ~15% better than V6, still unreliable for in-image typography
Decrypt called it 'the former industry standard struggles to keep up' — improvements are real but rivals caught up
Aesthetic quality and personalised style still unmatched for stylised art and illustration use cases
best_for: Stylised art, illustration, concept art where aesthetics dominate
Personalised image style via the personalisation feature (one of MJ's signature differentiators)
Mood / vibe-driven creative work where prompt nuance matters more than precision
Brand visual exploration and reference generation
not_ideal_for: Text-in-image rendering (typography, signage, posters with reliable letters)
Photoreal product / scene editing requiring precise reference adherence (FLUX.2 / Imagen 4 lead here)
API-only headless workflows — Midjourney is interactive-first via Discord and web app

sources.name: AI Art Force — V7 hands-on review

public_view.plain_english:
Midjourney's seventh-generation image model — released April 2025
on Discord and the new Midjourney web app. V7 still leads on
artistic aesthetic against any other text-to-image model: people
use it when they want a result that looks designed, not just
correct. V8 Alpha entered testing March 2026.
public_view.analogy:
Other image models are like having a competent illustrator who
follows the brief. Midjourney is the art director who interprets
the brief — sometimes inconveniently, but the output looks
intentional.
public_view.investment_angle:
Midjourney remains profitable on subscription with no investor
capital — a unicorn anomaly. Its moat is the curated aesthetic
style baked into the post-training data and the network of
millions of users whose prompts/upvotes refine that aesthetic.
Hard to replicate with weights alone.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Defines the "good-looking AI image" baseline that every other
model is judged against. Even with Flux.2 and Gemini Image
catching up technically, Midjourney leads on the subjective
artistic-output dimension that drives consumer use.

nodes.body_md:
## Why aesthetic still matters

Image generation has bifurcated:

- **Technical accuracy** (prompt adherence, text rendering, anatomy) — led by Reve, Ideogram, GPT Image 1.5, Flux.2
- **Aesthetic quality** (composition, color, mood, "looks designed") — led by Midjourney V7

For commercial design / marketing / concept-art workflows, aesthetic still wins.

nodes.title: Llama 4 — Meta's MoE Open Frontier

model_spec.architecture: Sparse MoE + native multimodal (early-fusion vision tokens)

feels_like: Meta's first MoE-native Llama and first natively multimodal open-weights generation
Maverick (17B active / 128 experts) beats GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 Flash on most benchmarks at release
Scout (17B active / 16 experts) fits in a single H100 with a claimed 10M-token context window
Benchmark numbers landed under a cloud — community questioned whether the LMArena score reflected the open-weight checkpoint
Real-world testers found gaps between announcement claims and independent reproduction
Despite controversy, the open weights gave the ecosystem a viable post-Llama-3 baseline
best_for: Self-hosted multimodal applications where API models can't go
Long-context retrieval and document QA (especially Scout's 10M window)
Fine-tuning on private data while staying inside Meta's open license
Cost-sensitive multimodal inference at scale
not_ideal_for: Frontier-leaderboard reasoning — Claude 4, GPT-5, DeepSeek R1 score higher
Edge / single-consumer-GPU deployments (even Scout needs an H100)
Workflows where the LMArena-style controversy is a credibility risk

public_view.plain_english:
Meta's first MoE-native Llama generation, released April 2025
with three sizes — Scout (109B / 17B active, 10M context),
Maverick (400B / 17B active), and Behemoth (~2T total, still in
preview at launch). Open weights but with stricter licensing
than Llama 3 (acceptable use policy expanded). Mixed reception:
benchmark scores trailed expectations vs internal Meta
commentary that had hyped Behemoth as "GPT-5 class."
public_view.analogy:
Meta's bet to keep pace with DeepSeek V3 / Qwen 2.5 / GPT-5
via architectural efficiency (MoE) rather than sheer parameter
count. Whether the bet paid off is contested — Llama 4 didn't
blow open the open-frontier the way Llama 3.1 405B did.

applications.product: WhatsApp / Instagram / Facebook Meta AI assistant

public_view.investment_angle:
Llama 4's mixed reception was a setback for the "Meta keeps
closing the open-vs-closed gap" narrative. While Llama 4 still
held the open-weight lead in some categories (Scout's 10M
context), DeepSeek V3 / R1 and Qwen 2.5 captured the
benchmark-leadership story. Meta's response in mid-2025 was a
reorganization — the "Llama 5 / superintelligence team" effort
— and recruitment war for top researchers. The competitive
dynamic suggests open-weight leadership has fragmented: Meta
holds Western open, Chinese labs hold global benchmark
leadership, no single lab owns the position.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Llama 4 illustrates that open-weight leadership is no longer a
single-lab story. Meta's structural advantages (compute, data,
reach) didn't translate to an obvious win in the generation
after Llama 3 — pointing to either a methodological gap or a
talent / strategy gap that Meta is now actively trying to close.

citations.title: The Llama 4 herd: The beginning of a new era of natively multimodal AI innovation

nodes.body_md:
## How Llama 4 compares (Q2 2025)

| Model | Date | Org | Active params | MMLU | Context | Open? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Llama 3.1 405B | 2024-07 | Meta | 405B | 88.6% | 128k | open |
| DeepSeek V3 | 2024-12 | DeepSeek | 37B | 88.5% | 128k | open |
| Qwen 2.5 72B | 2024-09 | Alibaba | 72B | 85.3% | 128k | open |
| **Llama 4 Maverick** | **2025-04** | **Meta** | **17B** | **~85%** | **1M** | **open** |
| **Llama 4 Scout** | **2025-04** | **Meta** | **17B** | **~80%** | **10M** | **open** |
| Claude Sonnet 4 | 2025-05 | Anthropic | undisclosed | — | 200k | API |
| Gemini 2.5 Pro | 2025-03 | Google | undisclosed | — | 1M | API |

Maverick's MMLU was lower than expected (the launch communications had implied frontier parity; benchmarks at release showed it modestly behind DeepSeek V3 on most non-context dimensions). Scout's 10M context was the unambiguous open-weight record.

nodes.title: Cohere Embed v4 — 128K Context Multimodal Embeddings

feels_like: Vision-language model from Cohere — see the linked sources below for benchmark and review coverage
Vision and multimodal tasks are the typical fit per the published model card
best_for: Vision tasks (charts, documents, images) per the model card
See the model spec and sources block for benchmarked use cases
not_ideal_for: Tasks far outside the modalities listed in this model's spec
Workflows where a more recent successor in the same family scores higher

public_view.plain_english:
Cohere's April 2025 embedding model — 128K context window (the
longest available), multilingual across 100+ languages, with
native support for binary quantization that shrinks vector size
10× without significant retrieval quality loss. Top of MTEB at
66.3 average score.
public_view.analogy:
Embeddings are the "library card catalog" of AI — they let you
find the right document for a query. Embed v4 has the longest
cards (128K context per item) and the smallest cards (binary
quantized) at the same time.
public_view.investment_angle:
Cohere has anchored on enterprise RAG and Embed v4 is the lever.
128K context per chunk + binary quantization changes the vector-
DB cost structure for legal / healthcare / financial document
workflows. Differentiator vs Voyage / OpenAI: longer context per
item.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Pushed embedding context windows from ~8K (OpenAI text-embedding-3)
to 128K — meaning entire chapters / contracts / cases can be
embedded as single vectors instead of split-and-aggregate. Changes
RAG architecture for long-document use cases.

nodes.body_md:
## Embeddings leaderboard (April 2026 MTEB)

| Model | Avg | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Google gemini-embedding-001 | 68.32 | Multimodal incl. video / audio / PDF |
| NVIDIA NV-Embed-v2 | 72.31 | Open weight |
| Qwen3-Embedding-8B | 70.58 | Open weight, multilingual |
| BGE-en-icl | 71.24 | Open weight |
| **Cohere Embed v4** | 66.3 | 128K context |
| OpenAI text-embedding-3-large | 64.6 | API only |

nodes.title: Kling 2.0 — Kuaishou's Text-to-Video Flagship

model_spec.architecture: DiT-style latent video diffusion + motion consistency training

benchmarks.name: Video · Video Arena Elo
benchmarks.vs_baseline: Rank #5 of 24 · Top #5 of 24 · AA · Video Arena

feels_like: AI filmmakers consistently rate Kling among the top 2 image-to-video tools, behind only Veo 3
Curious Refuge: 'Best image-to-video tool in 2025' with motion fluidity 'beats everyone except maybe Veo 3'
2.5 Turbo blind-test win rates 285% vs Seedance 1.0 mini, 212% vs Veo 3 Fast, 160% vs Seedance 1.0
2.6 (Dec 2025) added simultaneous audio-visual generation — semantic alignment of voice rhythm + visuals
5-second 1080p video at 25 credits in 2.5 Turbo — ~30% cheaper than 2.1
Strong character consistency and physical motion — preferred for cinematic-style storyboarding
best_for: Image-to-video workflows where motion quality matters most
Short-form social cinematic content with tight physical realism
Storyboard-to-shot pipelines for ad and entertainment work
Audio-synced video generation (2.6 model) at competitive credit cost
not_ideal_for: Long-form (>10s) coherent narrative shots — still capped at short clips
Self-hosted / open-weights workflows — Kling is hosted only
Highest-end photorealistic reels — Veo 3 still has the edge in some scenarios

public_view.plain_english:
Kuaishou's (Chinese short-video platform, TikTok competitor)
text-to-video flagship. Originally Kling 1.0 launched June 2024
before Sora was publicly available — briefly making it the only
production-accessible cinema-quality text-to-video product
anywhere. Kling 2.0 (April 2025) extended duration to 3
minutes and added image-to-video plus multi-shot coherence.
public_view.analogy:
The "Chinese Sora" that shipped before the American Sora —
Chinese short-video platforms moved from consumer product to
generative content pipeline faster than Western incumbents,
since their existing user base was already video-native.

applications.product: Integrated in Meitu / Capcut-style editing apps

public_view.investment_angle:
Kling's strategic importance: it's the first Chinese-origin
AI product where the global launch preceded the Western
competitor's launch in the same category. By Q1 2025, Kling
had become the most-used text-to-video product globally by
generated-clip count (exceeding Runway + Pika combined) —
largely driven by Chinese mobile users but with growing
Western creator adoption. Kuaishou's commercial model
(freemium + credits) is more consumer-friendly than
Sora / Veo's Pro-subscription-gated access, which broadens
the user base even where quality lags.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Kling represents a structural shift: for the first time in
a major generative AI category, the first-mover advantage
went to a Chinese platform rather than a US lab. The
category (text-to-video) may permanently bifurcate into
US-flagship-quality and Chinese-platform-scale segments.

nodes.body_md:
## Kling lineage

| Version | Date | Key capability |
|---|---|---|
| Kling 1.0 | 2024-06 | 2 minutes, 1080p; first global-access video-gen product at quality |
| Kling 1.5 | 2024-09 | higher resolution, better motion |
| Kling 1.6 | 2024-12 | image-to-video + motion control |
| **Kling 2.0** | **2025-04** | **3 minutes + multi-shot coherence** |
| Kling 2.1 Master | 2025-Q3 | further quality and duration improvements |

## Text-to-video competitive landscape (2024-25)

| Product | 2024 share of generated video clips | 2025 trajectory |
|---|---|---|
| Runway Gen-2/3 | ~20% | declining share |
| Pika | ~15% | declining |
| Sora | ~10% (delayed launch) | growing (post-Dec 2024 release) |
| **Kling** | **~35%** | **continuing lead on clip count** |
| Luma Dream Machine | ~10% | growing (US specialty) |
| Veo | ~5% | growing |
| Hailuo (MiniMax) | ~5% | growing (China) |

Kling's share by clip count substantially exceeds its share by "cinema-quality output" — i.e., many of its clips are casual-consumer rather than professional. This matches Kuaishou's broader consumer-video-platform positioning.

nodes.title: OpenAI o3 — Frontier Reasoning at Scale

model_spec.architecture: Decoder-only transformer + RL on reasoning + native tool use during thinking

feels_like: Step-change on ARC-AGI-1 — 75.7% at the public $10K compute limit, 87.5% at high compute (172x)
But ARC-AGI-2 (March 2025) cratered the narrative: <3% on the harder benchmark vs 60% for the average human
The released April-2025 o3 is NOT the December preview — preview had ARC-AGI-1 in its training, public version is more honest
TechCrunch reported the released o3 scored lower than initial OpenAI claims implied
Reasoning trace is much faster than o1 and the honesty/calibration of the chain improved noticeably
Still expensive at high effort; still slow on the hardest tasks
best_for: Frontier reasoning research and benchmark exploration
Hard math (AIME, IMO-style) and graduate-level science (GPQA)
Code that needs novel algorithm design rather than scaffolded refactors
Tool-using agents that benefit from longer reasoning windows
not_ideal_for: Latency-sensitive chat — o3-mini or GPT-4o are far faster
Tasks framed slightly outside training distribution — Apple's variable-rename study still applies
Bulk inference — high-effort o3 is one of the most expensive options on the market

sources.name: ARC Prize — o3 breakthrough on ARC-AGI-Pub
TechCrunch — o3 scores lower than initially implied

public_view.plain_english:
OpenAI's full o3 release in April 2025, after a December 2024
teaser. Step-change on math, code, and frontier reasoning
benchmarks; the first model to convincingly beat the ARC-AGI
visual-reasoning challenge (long held up as a "true general
intelligence" benchmark). Native tool use during reasoning —
the model decides mid-thought to run code, search the web,
or call external APIs.
public_view.analogy:
Where o1 was "the model thinks before it answers," o3 is "the
model thinks, executes code, reads the web, double-checks
itself, and then answers." Inference cost per query rose
proportionally — flagship o3-pro queries can run $1-10 each.
public_view.investment_angle:
o3's release answered the strategic question DeepSeek R1 had
posed: can closed labs maintain meaningful capability leadership
over open competitors? On benchmark capability, yes — o3 is
measurably ahead of R1 / Gemini 2.5 Pro / Claude 3.7 on the
hardest reasoning evaluations as of release. On cost-adjusted
capability, the answer is more contested — a $50 o3 query and
a $0.50 R1 query will both solve most non-frontier tasks.
OpenAI's revenue trajectory in 2025 ($10B+ ARR by mid-year,
~$15B by end of year) was driven heavily by o3-tier
consumption — particularly the $200/mo ChatGPT Pro tier
(which had crossed 5M paying subscribers by Q3 2025).
public_view.why_it_matters:
o3 set the high-water mark for frontier capability in 2025 and
became the reference benchmark for "what's the best AI can
do?" debates in policy, education, and labor-market analysis.
The ARC-AGI result specifically reignited the AGI-timeline
discussion across AI safety circles.

nodes.body_md:
## o3 vs reasoning peers (Q2 2025)

| Model | Date | AIME | GPQA | ARC-AGI | HLE | API $ / 1M out |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| o1 | 2024-09 | 83.3% | 78.0% | 25% | — | $60 |
| DeepSeek R1 | 2025-01 | 79.8% | 71.5% | 15% | 8.5% | $2.19 |
| Gemini 2.5 Pro | 2025-03 | 92.0% | 84.0% | — | 18.8% | $5-21 |
| Claude 3.7 Sonnet | 2025-02 | 80.0% | 84.8% | — | 8.9% | $15 |
| **o3** | **2025-04** | **96.7%** | **87.7%** | **75.7%** | **20.3%** | **~$60** |
| Claude 4 Opus | 2025-05 | — | 83.3% | — | — | $75 |

The ARC-AGI gap (o3's 75.7% vs nearest peer ~25%) was the single most discussed result in AI of the first half of 2025. The benchmark's creator (François Chollet) revised his framing of what ARC-AGI measured in light of the result.

## Inference economics

o3 querying the high-compute "high effort" mode reportedly costs OpenAI $20-2000 per task (per the ARC-AGI benchmark write-up). The consumer-tier o3 in ChatGPT Pro uses much shorter reasoning traces (~$1-5 per query economic equivalent), with throughput limits.

This established a new pricing architecture: API tiers stratified by both model AND inference-compute budget per query, not just model alone.

nodes.title: GPT Image 1 / 1.5 — OpenAI's Image Frontier

feels_like: First natively multimodal image model — text and image inputs share the same transformer backbone, not a separate diffusion stack
130M users created 700M images in the first week of the ChatGPT image rollout — extreme product-market fit
Excels at text rendering, prompt adherence, and using GPT-4o's world-knowledge for grounded composition
Still struggles with non-Latin scripts (Chinese / Japanese / Arabic) and small in-image text
Available via ChatGPT, the Images 2.0 product, and the Responses API as gpt-image-1
Successor GPT Image 1.5 (Dec 2025) generates 4x faster — original now feels slow at 10-30s per image
best_for: Conversational image generation inside ChatGPT — context carries from prompt to prompt
Marketing graphics that need readable English text in the image
Image editing with reference inputs (the multimodal input is a real product feature)
Workflows that want to use one OpenAI account for text + image without juggling APIs
not_ideal_for: Languages using non-Latin characters where text-rendering hallucinates
Latency-sensitive bulk generation (10-30s per image; competitors are faster)
Pure aesthetic / illustration work where Midjourney V7 still has stylistic edge

sources.name: OpenAI — Introducing image generation in the API
InfoQ — Improved image generation in GPT-4o

public_view.plain_english:
OpenAI's image generator integrated directly into ChatGPT and the
Responses API — released as gpt-image-1 in April 2025 and refined
to gpt-image-1.5 in late 2025. Excels at prompt adherence,
in-image text rendering, and style transfer. Currently leads the
Artificial Analysis image arena at ~1264 Elo.
public_view.analogy:
DALL-E 3's successor that learned to actually READ the prompt.
Where DALL-E often missed details, gpt-image-1 nails specific
layouts, text, and reference styles.
public_view.investment_angle:
Image generation in ChatGPT drives massive consumer engagement
(the "Studio Ghibli" trend in early 2025 single-handedly added
millions of users). At the API layer, gpt-image-1 is OpenAI's
answer to Adobe Firefly for commercial-safe image generation
with audit trails.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Replaced DALL-E as the OpenAI image model and reasserted
OpenAI's frontier position after a year of MidJourney and Flux
leading. Demonstrated that backbone-shared multimodal models
beat single-modality image specialists on prompt adherence.

nodes.body_md:
## What's distinctive

- **Native ChatGPT integration** — image gen is just another response type, not a separate tool
- **Reference image input** — pass a photo, ask the model to restyle it, edit it, or extend it
- **Best-in-class text rendering** — readable signs, posters, UI mockups
- **C2PA + watermarking** for commercial trust

nodes.title: Qwen 3 — Toggleable Thinking, 119 Languages

model_spec.architecture: Decoder-only transformer + MoE with toggleable thinking mode

feels_like: Best open-source reasoning model at its release — 235B-A22B (Thinking) beats DeepSeek-R1 on 17/23 benchmarks
Toggle-able thinking mode: same weights serve both reasoning and fast-chat modes
Strong 119-language coverage; the most genuinely multilingual frontier-tier model
Coder variant reaches 77.2% on SWE-bench Verified — competitive with Claude 4.5 Opus's 80.9%
GPQA Diamond 87.8% and AIME26 94.1% — frontier reasoning at open-weights pricing
Apache-2.0 license + 1M-context coder variant make it production-ready, not a research toy
best_for: Multilingual production deployments (119 languages) where most models stay English-centric
Self-hosted reasoning workflows that need both 'fast mode' and 'thinking mode' from one weight set
Open-weights agentic coding (Qwen3-Coder) with very large context windows
Cost-sensitive bulk reasoning that would be prohibitive on closed APIs
not_ideal_for: Multimodal tasks (Qwen3 base is text — vision lives in Qwen3-VL, audio in Qwen3-Audio)
Edge / single-consumer-GPU deployments at the 235B scale
Workflows where Western-platform compatibility is a contractual requirement

public_view.plain_english:
Alibaba's open-weight Qwen 3 family covers everything from a tiny
0.6B model to a 235B mixture-of-experts. Every size has a switch
that turns "thinking" mode on or off — same weights, two behaviors.
Speaks 119 languages and was the first big open release to match
DeepSeek-R1 on reasoning benchmarks.
public_view.analogy:
Like a car with eco / sport modes — the Qwen3 weights swap between
fast direct answers and slow deliberate chain-of-thought reasoning,
no second model required.
public_view.investment_angle:
Qwen 3 is the credible open alternative to DeepSeek for enterprises
that want hosted inference with Apache 2.0 weights. The toggleable
thinking mode means one deployment serves both cheap chat and
expensive reasoning — collapsing a tier of model ops. Alibaba is
using Qwen as anchor for Bailian Cloud, the same way Anthropic
anchors AWS Bedrock and Google anchors Vertex.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Demonstrated that the open-weight Chinese ecosystem could match
Western closed reasoning models within months of o3 and R1, on
permissive licenses, across the entire size spectrum from edge
(0.6B) to frontier (235B MoE).

nodes.body_md:
## Why it mattered

Three things at once:

1. **Toggleable thinking** — `enable_thinking=True/False` in the chat template switches the same weights between R1-style chain-of-thought and direct answers. No second model.
2. **119 languages** — overtook Llama 3 on multilingual coverage; only Aya Expanse (Cohere) matched it for non-English breadth.
3. **Open-weight reasoning parity** — first non-DeepSeek open release to reach o1-mini / DeepSeek-R1 levels on AIME, LiveCodeBench, and GPQA.

The April 2025 release made it impossible to argue that "real reasoning models are closed-source only."

public_view.plain_english:
NVIDIA's open speech-recognition family — Parakeet-TDT-0.6B
currently tops the Hugging Face Open ASR Leaderboard at 6.05%
word error rate. The Token-Duration Transducer architecture is
cheap to deploy (single-GPU realtime) and licensed permissively
via NVIDIA's NeMo stack.
public_view.analogy:
Whisper opened up open-source transcription in 2022. Parakeet is
the next leap — same open-source story, lower error rate, faster
inference.
public_view.investment_angle:
NVIDIA's NeMo open-source stack (Parakeet ASR + Canary multilingual
+ Cosmos world models) is part of their "give away the software,
sell the hardware" play. Parakeet keeps NVIDIA upstream of every
speech AI pipeline.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Replaced Whisper as the default open-source ASR for many
production deployments. Combined with the Canary-Qwen-2.5B
record (5.63% WER) released months later, NVIDIA owns the open
ASR frontier in 2025–2026.

nodes.title: Cartesia Sonic — Sub-100ms TTS

feels_like: Audio model from Cartesia — see the linked sources below for benchmark and review coverage
Audio synthesis or transcription per the published model card
best_for: Audio synthesis / transcription tasks per the model card
See the model spec and sources block for benchmarked use cases
not_ideal_for: Tasks far outside the modalities listed in this model's spec
Workflows where a more recent successor in the same family scores higher

public_view.plain_english:
Cartesia's text-to-speech model — Sonic-3 hits 40–90 millisecond
time-to-first-audio, the fastest in production. Built on
state-space-model architecture (the team came out of Mamba's
research group at Stanford), it's optimized for live voice agents
and call-center deployments where latency kills conversation
quality.
public_view.analogy:
ElevenLabs is the Rolls-Royce — best sound. Cartesia is the
Formula 1 — fast enough that the conversation feels real-time.
Different optima for different jobs.
public_view.investment_angle:
Voice agents (replacing call centers, ordering, scheduling) are
the highest-revenue voice AI category. Latency is the bottleneck
— 200ms+ feels like a delay, 100ms+ feels natural. Cartesia's
sub-100ms makes voice agents feel like talking to a person, not
waiting for a bot. Raised $80M+ on this thesis.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Made low-latency TTS competitive with quality-tier alternatives.
Cartesia is the reason voice agents stopped feeling laggy in
2024–2025.

nodes.body_md:
## Why latency matters for voice

In a phone conversation, humans expect responses within ~250ms. Earlier TTS pipelines (200ms TTFA + 200ms LLM + 100ms ASR) blew that budget. Cartesia at 40-90ms TTFA leaves headroom for the LLM to think, keeping the perceived response time under the natural-conversation threshold.

nodes.title: Veo 3 — Google's Native-Audio Video Model

model_spec.architecture: Latent diffusion DiT + native joint audio-video generation

benchmarks.name: Video · Video Arena Elo
benchmarks.vs_baseline: Rank #9 of 24 · Top #9 of 24 · AA · Video Arena

feels_like: First Google video model with native synced audio — dialogue, effects, ambient soundscape from one model
Sound is generated from raw pixels of the video itself, not stitched as a post-process
Lip-syncing and dubbing are the standout strength — realistic conversation in generated scenes
Up to 8s clips in 1080p; some configurations support 4K — covers draft and finished-quality use cases
Audio sync only works on first attempt for ~25% of generations — needs iteration
Best at scene composition / camera direction when prompts are detailed and structured
best_for: Cinematic short-form content where audio integration matters (ads, trailers, social)
Character dialogue scenes — best lip-sync of any major video model at release
Storyboard-to-finished-shot pipelines via Vertex AI for studio workflows
Replacing two-model stacks (video + voice generation) with one
not_ideal_for: Long-form (>8s) coherent narrative shots
Frame-perfect creative control — limited keyframe editing vs Runway / Kling
Workloads where the 25% audio-on-first-try hit rate is unacceptable

sources.name: TechCrunch — Veo 3 generates videos and soundtracks

public_view.plain_english:
Google's third-generation text-to-video model, released May
2025 and integrated into Gemini and Google Vertex AI by
summer. Headline capability: native synchronized audio
generation (speech, music, sound effects) at the same time
as the video — beating Sora 2 (which added native audio
in September 2025) to that capability by 4 months.
public_view.analogy:
Google's "we still have native multimodal" reminder. After
being slow to ship reasoning (Gemini 2.5 Pro), Google
moved fast on video — leveraging YouTube's 800M+ hours of
training video and DeepMind's accumulated diffusion
research.
public_view.investment_angle:
Veo 3 is the strongest "Google's vertical integration matters"
proof point in 2025. YouTube + DeepMind + TPU together let
Google ship a text-to-video model with audio that no
individual capability would have achieved alone. Whether
Google can convert this into market leadership in the
advertising / content-production segment depends partly on
Veo's productization (Flow targeting professional filmmakers
is the deliberate move) and partly on broader trust /
distribution dynamics.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Veo 3 demonstrated that Google's model labs could ship at
the frontier of a major modality on Google's calendar, not
in response to OpenAI. The shipping capability matters more
than the specific Veo 3 release for the longer-term
competitive positioning.

nodes.body_md:
## Veo lineage

| Version | Date | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Veo 1 | 2024-05 | 60s 1080p, demo only |
| Veo 2 | 2024-12 | 8s 4k, API-accessible |
| **Veo 3** | **2025-05** | **native audio + improved physics + production access** |
| Veo 3.1 / 4 | 2025-Q4+ | continued iteration |

## Veo 3 vs Sora 2 — feature parity timeline

| Feature | Veo 3 (May 2025) | Sora 2 (Sep 2025) |
|---|---|---|
| Native audio | ✓ (first) | ✓ (4 months later) |
| Output duration | 8s baseline / longer experimental | 20s |
| Resolution | 4k available (premium) | 1080p baseline |
| Physics quality | strong | strong (slightly better in some cases) |
| Distribution | API + Gemini app | Sora consumer app + ChatGPT |

The competitive dynamic in late 2025 saw Sora and Veo trading capability leads — neither dominating across all dimensions. Real adoption split by use case: professional filmmaking → Veo (via Flow); casual consumer → Sora (via standalone app); Chinese market → Kling.

nodes.title: Claude 4 — Opus & Sonnet (Agentic Frontier Family)

feels_like: Sustains hours-long agent loops without losing the thread — testers ran Opus 4 on 7+ hour autonomous coding sessions
Best-in-class for real-world software engineering at release; SWE-bench Verified gains over Claude 3.7 Sonnet
Native MCP support makes tool-using agents noticeably more reliable in production
File editing and computer use feel meaningfully better than 3.7 — fewer 'almost works' debugging cycles
Opus 4 is slower and more expensive than alternatives; reserve for the hardest tasks
Sonnet 4 is the workhorse — same agentic strengths at a much friendlier price
best_for: AI-software-engineer products (Devin, Cursor, Cline) that need long-horizon autonomy
Agentic workflows with tool use, file editing, and computer use
Complex bug fixes that span multiple files / systems
Multi-hour research or coding sessions where context retention matters
not_ideal_for: High-volume chat or simple Q&A (use Sonnet or Haiku)
Strictly latency-sensitive applications — Opus is slower
Fully offline / air-gapped deployments (API only)

public_view.plain_english:
Anthropic's first model family explicitly designed for sustained
autonomous work — running for hours on multi-step tasks without
human intervention. Opus 4 became the dominant model behind
"AI software engineer" products in 2025, while Sonnet 4 stayed
the workhorse mid-tier. Both shipped with native tool use, file
editing, and computer-use improvements.
public_view.analogy:
Earlier models were assistants — they helped you do work. Claude
4 was an associate — you'd give it a task in the morning and
review its commits in the afternoon. The unit of work shifted
from "one prompt response" to "one autonomous session."
public_view.investment_angle:
Claude 4 marks the transition from "AI as autocomplete" to "AI as
junior engineer." The economic implication: a single Claude Code
session at $5-20 cost output that previously required a junior
engineering hour (~$80 fully-loaded). This 4-16× compression at
the unit-of-work level is what's reshaping engineering team
structure at AI-native startups (Cursor reportedly hit $500M ARR
with under 100 employees by mid-2025). Anthropic's ARR reportedly
crossed $4B annualized by Q3 2025, with developer-tools API
revenue >50% of the total. The "AI labor substitution" macro
thesis gets its first hard datapoints from this model generation.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Claude 4 is the model that made "have AI write code, autonomously,
and you review the PR" a normal workflow at frontier engineering
teams. The labor-market implications — entry-level engineering
hiring contraction visible in Q3 2025 BLS data — are downstream
of this and similar releases.

nodes.body_md:
## How Claude 4 compares to mid-2025 frontier

| Model | Date | Org | SWE-bench Verified | TerminalBench | GPQA | MMLU |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude 3.7 Sonnet | 2025-02 | Anthropic | 70.3% | 27.6% | 84.8% | — |
| GPT-4.5 | 2025-02 | OpenAI | — | — | 71.4% | — |
| o3 | 2025-04 | OpenAI | 71.7% | 30.2% | 87.7% | — |
| Gemini 2.5 Pro | 2025-03 | Google | 63.8% | 25.3% | 84.0% | — |
| **Claude Sonnet 4** | **2025-05** | **Anthropic** | **73.6%** | **35.5%** | **75.4%** | — |
| **Claude Opus 4** | **2025-05** | **Anthropic** | **79.4%** | **43.2%** | **83.3%** | — |
| Llama 4 Maverick | 2025-04 | Meta | — | — | — | — |
| DeepSeek V3 / R1.5 | 2025-Q2 | DeepSeek | — | — | — | — |

Opus 4's SWE-bench Verified score (79.4%) was the largest single-release jump on that benchmark since the Claude 3.5 → 3.7 transition. TerminalBench (which evaluates terminal-use capability — the closest proxy for "can it operate a developer environment") more than doubled from 3.7 Sonnet.

## The agentic delta

Real-world agentic capability isn't fully captured by static benchmarks. Anecdotal but consistent reports from production users in mid-2025:

- Claude Code sessions complete 60-80% of well-scoped engineering tasks end-to-end with Opus 4, vs 30-50% with 3.7 Sonnet.
- Mean time-to-drift (when the agent loses the thread) extended from ~45 min to ~3 hours.
- Cost per completed task dropped despite Opus 4 being priced higher than Sonnet 3.7 — fewer retries, shorter total context.

These deltas are why Cursor, Windsurf, and Devin all defaulted to Opus 4 within weeks of release.

## Successor: Opus 4.6, Opus 4.7

Anthropic shipped Opus 4.5 (October 2025), 4.6 (December 2025), and 4.7 (early 2026) on a roughly 8-week cadence — each a substantial capability and reliability update without re-incrementing the major version. The 4.6 release added the 1M-token context window variant currently powering many long-document and large-codebase workflows.

By early 2026 the Opus 4.x line is the de facto default for any agentic-AI workflow that requires sustained reasoning, tool use, and multi-hour autonomy.

public_view.plain_english:
ElevenLabs' June 2025 flagship voice model — generates speech
that conveys emotion, accents, and conversational pacing. Used to
require professional voice actors and a recording studio; with
v3, a 5-second sample is enough to clone a voice that sounds
natural across paragraphs of varied content. Conversational v3
(Feb 2026) added live agent capability.
public_view.analogy:
Earlier TTS sounds like a navigation system — flat, robotic.
Eleven v3 is closer to actually hiring a voice actor — it shouts,
laughs, whispers, and pauses where a human would.
public_view.investment_angle:
ElevenLabs is the category-defining voice infrastructure
company — used by audiobook publishers, video creators, game
studios, and increasingly customer-service voice agents. Raised
$180M+ at $3B+ valuation. Network effect: the larger their voice
library, the better any new voice clone sounds.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Made convincing synthetic voice ubiquitous. Combined with
OpenAI's Realtime API and Google's Gemini Live, v3 anchors the
transition from text-first to voice-first AI products.

nodes.title: ERNIE 4.5 — Baidu Goes Open

model_spec.parameters: Up to 424B total / 47B active MoE; smaller dense variants from 0.3B

feels_like: Vision-language model from Baidu — see the linked sources below for benchmark and review coverage
Tool-use and agent loops are the typical fit per the published model card

See the model spec and sources block for benchmarked use cases
not_ideal_for: Tasks far outside the modalities listed in this model's spec
Workflows where a more recent successor in the same family scores higher

public_view.plain_english:
Baidu's June 2025 strategy reversal — they open-sourced ten
variants of ERNIE 4.5 under Apache 2.0, from 0.3B all the way to a
424B mixture-of-experts. Until then, Baidu had kept ERNIE closed
while DeepSeek, Qwen, and GLM defined the open-weight Chinese
landscape. ERNIE 4.5 was Baidu's catch-up move.
public_view.analogy:
Microsoft's "embrace, extend, extinguish" inverted — Baidu spent
years insisting closed APIs were the right model, then conceded
the open-weight ground in a single release.
public_view.investment_angle:
Baidu's pivot to Apache 2.0 was forced by the market — open-weight
Chinese models had eaten its enterprise share. ERNIE X1 (reasoning,
March 2025) and ERNIE-4.5-VL (vision-thinking) round out the
product line. Baidu Cloud is now a hosting layer rather than a
model moat.
public_view.why_it_matters:
The shift from Baidu-closed to Baidu-open is the most visible
capitulation to the DeepSeek-led open-weight wave. It means EVERY
major Chinese hyperscaler (Alibaba, ByteDance via Doubao, Baidu,
Tencent, Huawei) now ships open-weight models at frontier scale.

All Apache 2.0.

nodes.title: Kimi K2 — Moonshot's Open MoE Reasoning

model_spec.architecture: Sparse MoE + extended-context attention + reasoning post-training

benchmarks.name: Vision · MMMU-Pro
benchmarks.vs_baseline: Rank #9 of 26 · Top #9 of 26 · Artificial Analysis

feels_like: 1T-parameter MoE / 32B active per token — Moonshot's open-weights debut, modified MIT license
65.8% on SWE-bench Verified single-attempt — outperforms every model tested except Claude Sonnet 4 at release
53.7% on LiveCodeBench v6 — strong open-source coding tier
75.1% on GPQA Diamond — beats GPT-4.1 (66.3%) and Gemini 2.5 Flash (68.2%)
Pre-trained on 15.5T tokens with zero training instabilities — the largest stable open MoE training to date
Set up for agentic workflows — designed around tool-use, not just chat
best_for: Self-hosted agent platforms where API models can't go (regulated, private cloud)
Cost-sensitive frontier-tier inference via budget providers
Coding agents that need long context + open weights for fine-tuning
Chinese/multilingual production deployments where Western platforms are blocked
not_ideal_for: Edge / single-GPU deployments — 1T MoE still demands multi-node serving
Multimodal tasks (text-only at this generation; vision lives in Kimi-VL)
Latency-sensitive interactive chat at the full model scale

sources.name: Cline — Kimi K2 for coding first impressions
Data Science Dojo — Kimi K2 deep dive

public_view.plain_english:
Moonshot AI's first open-weight foundation model, released July
2025 — a strategic pivot from the closed-only Kimi consumer
product positioning. Trillion-parameter MoE, 32B active,
competitive with DeepSeek V3 on most benchmarks and with
Claude Sonnet 4 on agentic coding. Brought Moonshot back into
the open-frontier conversation after a difficult 2024 of
consumer-product user growth challenges.
public_view.analogy:
Moonshot's "we are still in the open game" announcement.
After 18 months of Kimi consumer-product positioning that
plateaued, K2 reset the company narrative around foundation-
model capability that any developer could download.

applications.product: Kimi K2 hosted on Moonshot API

public_view.investment_angle:
Kimi K2's release was Moonshot's recovery move after a
difficult 2024. The company had raised >$1B at >$3B
valuation in early 2024 on consumer Kimi traction; by late
2024, paid user retention had plateaued and the AI tigers
competitive landscape had hardened. K2 reset the technical
narrative: even if consumer Kimi wasn't dominant, Moonshot
could ship at the open-frontier alongside DeepSeek and
Qwen. By 2026, Moonshot's valuation had stabilized at
around the $3B level (vs the $10B+ trajectory implied by
early Kimi growth).
public_view.why_it_matters:
K2 demonstrates that Chinese open-frontier labs are not
just DeepSeek and Qwen — Moonshot, Zhipu, MiniMax,
Baichuan, and Stepfun all maintain meaningful open-weight
presence in 2025-26. The "Chinese open ecosystem" is a
multi-lab phenomenon, not a single-vendor story.

nodes.body_md:

| Qwen 2.5 72B | 2024-09 | Alibaba | 72B | 85.3% | — | Apache 2.0 |
| DeepSeek V3 | 2024-12 | DeepSeek | 37B | 88.5% | ~67% | open |
| Llama 4 Maverick | 2025-04 | Meta | 17B (400B total) | ~85% | — | open commercial |
| **Kimi K2** | **2025-07** | **Moonshot** | **~32B (~1T total)** | **~89%** | **~75%** | **open** |
| DeepSeek V3.5 | 2025-Q3 | DeepSeek | 37B | ~89% | — | open |
| Qwen 3 | 2025-Q2 | Alibaba | varied | — | — | open |

K2's SWE-bench Verified score (~75%) is one of the highest open-weight scores at release, briefly leading until Qwen 3 and DeepSeek V3.5 successors caught up.

nodes.title: Qwen3-Coder — Open-Source Coding Agent Frontier

feels_like: Coding-specialist variant — large-context coder.
Best open-source reasoning model at its release — 235B-A22B (Thinking) beats DeepSeek-R1 on 17/23 benchmarks
Toggle-able thinking mode: same weights serve both reasoning and fast-chat modes
Strong 119-language coverage; the most genuinely multilingual frontier-tier model
best_for: Multilingual production deployments (119 languages) where most models stay English-centric
Self-hosted reasoning workflows that need both 'fast mode' and 'thinking mode' from one weight set
Open-weights agentic coding (Qwen3-Coder) with very large context windows
not_ideal_for: Multimodal tasks (Qwen3 base is text — vision lives in Qwen3-VL, audio in Qwen3-Audio)
Edge / single-consumer-GPU deployments at the 235B scale

public_view.plain_english:
Qwen3-Coder is Alibaba's specialized coding-agent variant of Qwen 3,
fine-tuned on real software-engineering traces. It edits code across
repositories up to 1 million tokens long and can drive a terminal,
not just complete a single function. Released open-weights under
Apache 2.0 — the strongest open coding model of mid-2025.
public_view.analogy:
Most coding models are autocomplete. Qwen3-Coder is closer to a
junior engineer with a checked-out repo open in their editor — it
reads the whole codebase before suggesting an edit, then runs the
tests.
public_view.investment_angle:
Coding is the highest-revenue LLM use case and the one where open
weights threaten Cursor / Copilot economics most directly. Qwen3-
Coder closed the gap to Claude Sonnet 4 to ~3 SWE-bench points
while shipping fully open. Self-hosted enterprises (banks,
governments, hyperscalers) now have a real local-deployment
coding option.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Made "agentic coding under Apache 2.0" a real product, not a
benchmark game. Cursor, Cline, Roo, and Aider all shipped Qwen3-
Coder integrations within weeks — the first open model to drive
serious agentic coding workflows.

citations.title: Qwen3-Coder: Agentic Coding in the World

nodes.body_md:
## Why it matters

Agentic coding requires three things working together:

1. Long context (whole repos, not single files)
2. Tool use (file edits, shell, tests)
3. Multi-turn planning

Qwen3-Coder is the first open model to ship all three competently. It became the de-facto open backbone for self-hosted Copilot replacements within a quarter of release.

public_view.plain_english:
StepFun's July 2025 vision-language model — 321B parameters in a
mixture-of-experts arrangement, native multimodal from training
rather than bolted on. Released open-weight, with later Step-3.5-
Flash adding speed and Step-Audio variants extending into voice.
public_view.analogy:
A camera-and-microphone-equipped Qwen — same MoE architecture
pattern but designed multimodal-first.
public_view.investment_angle:
StepFun is the smaller player in the Chinese frontier wave — but
Step-3 plus the Step-Audio family (R1, R1.1 Realtime, EditX)
makes them the only Chinese open lab covering frontier text +
vision + voice in one stack. Targets vertical multimodal
applications: education, agents, voice IVR replacement.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Demonstrates that Chinese model labs are differentiating now —
StepFun on multimodal, MiniMax on inference cost, Moonshot on
long-horizon agents, Zhipu on coding. Not a single race anymore.

nodes.body_md:
## What it shipped

- 321B / 38B active MoE — first frontier-scale open vision MoE from China outside Alibaba
- Step-3.5-Flash (Feb 2026, Apache 2.0) — speed-tuned variant
- Step-Audio 2 / R1 / R1.1 Realtime — voice-stack siblings
- StepFun-Formalizer — math-formalization specialist

public_view.plain_english:
Zhipu's GLM-4.5 is a complete open-weight family — frontier MoE,
a smaller "Air" tier, and a vision variant — released July 2025
under the MIT license. By the time GLM-4.6 shipped in late 2025
(trained on Chinese Cambricon chips, no Nvidia), the family was
at parity with closed Western coding models on WebDev Arena.
public_view.analogy:
Zhipu played the role for China that Anthropic plays for the US —
quietly shipping a competitive frontier model with strong coding
and tool use, but doing it under MIT instead of behind an API.

applications.product: Hosted via OpenRouter, Together, Hugging Face

public_view.investment_angle:
GLM-4.6 is reportedly the first frontier-tier model trained on
domestic Chinese accelerators (Cambricon). If accurate, it's a
proof point that the Nvidia hardware moat is breachable, not just
in theory. Strategic implication: Western export controls are
less of a brake on the Chinese open-weight curve than expected
in 2023.
public_view.why_it_matters:
GLM-4.7 (Dec 2025) became the first non-Western model to top
WebDev Arena. The family's combination of MIT licensing, Chinese
hardware, and frontier coding scores marks a turning point in the
geopolitical model landscape.

citations.title: GLM-4.5: Reasoning, Coding, and Agentic Capabilities

nodes.body_md:
## Family timeline

| Date | Model | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Jul 2025 | GLM-4.5 / 4.5-Air | Initial open frontier; 355B MoE flagship |
| Aug 2025 | GLM-4.5V (106B vision) | First open vision model from Zhipu |
| Late 2025 | GLM-4.6 | Reportedly trained on Cambricon (Chinese) chips |
| Dec 22 2025 | **GLM-4.7** | #1 on WebDev Arena |
| Feb 2026 | "Pony Alpha" (suspected GLM-5) | Anonymous OpenRouter listing; rumored 745B/44B on Huawei Ascend |

## What's distinctive

- **MIT-licensed weights** — most permissive open license at frontier scale
- **Domestic hardware** — GLM-4.6's Cambricon training story (if validated) breaks a key Western talking point
- **Coding + tool use focus** — explicitly trained for agent workflows; WebDev Arena win reflects this
- **Tied to Tsinghua KEG** — academic + commercial dual identity, similar to DeepSeek's role

public_view.plain_english:
DeepMind's August 2025 world model — generates a 720p / 24fps
walkable, controllable world from a single image prompt, with
minutes of temporal coherence. Move forward, turn, pick up
objects — Genie 3 generates the next frame in real time based
on your action. The first generative world model that's actually
a playable environment, not a passive video.
public_view.analogy:
Sora generates a video clip — passive. Genie 3 generates a video
GAME world — you walk around inside it. Closer to a holodeck
than a film.
public_view.investment_angle:
World models are the upstream layer for embodied AI training —
safer and cheaper than real-world data collection for robotics,
autonomous driving, and game design. Genie 3 + V-JEPA 2 + Cosmos
+ Wayve GAIA define the category. The economic question: do
embodied agents train on real-world data or in synthetic worlds?
World models bet on the latter.
public_view.why_it_matters:
First credible interactive world model at production fidelity —
a category that includes Decart's Oasis (Minecraft), Microsoft
Muse, Wayve GAIA-2 (driving), and World Labs Marble (3D scenes).
Sets the trajectory for "AI generates the simulator, not just
the agent."

Workflows where a more recent successor in the same family scores higher

public_view.plain_english:
OpenAI's first open-weight language models since GPT-2 in 2019 —
a 20B and 120B Apache-2.0 release in August 2025. gpt-oss is
OpenAI's concession that the open ecosystem (Llama, Qwen,
DeepSeek) had become too important to ignore strategically.
public_view.analogy:
Like Apple finally releasing iWork as open source — a company
that had defined itself by closed products yielding ground to
keep developers in its ecosystem.
public_view.investment_angle:
Strategic concession more than a competitive shot — gpt-oss-120B
is solid but not best-in-class against Qwen3-235B-A22B or
DeepSeek-V3.2. OpenAI's goal: keep developers in OpenAI tooling
(responses API, agents SDK, evals) even when they want open
weights. Distribution-focused play, not a benchmark race.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Removed OpenAI's "no open weights" position as a talking point
against the closed-API model. Every Western frontier lab now
ships at least some open models (Anthropic the holdout). The
open/closed line is now PRODUCT TIER, not company identity.

nodes.title: GPT-5 — OpenAI's Unified Successor

model_spec.architecture: Unified routing model — switches internally between fast and reasoning modes

feels_like: Set a new ceiling on Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (68 at High effort) at release
94.6% on AIME 2025 without tools — math reasoning ahead of any prior frontier model
74.9% SWE-bench Verified — strong but trails Claude Opus 4.5's 80.9% on coding
23x token-cost spread between Minimal and High reasoning effort — pick effort by task carefully
Comparable to or better than human experts in roughly half of cases across 40 occupations
Pro tier with extended reasoning sets state-of-the-art on GPQA at 88.4% without tools
best_for: Hard math, science, and competition-style reasoning
Multi-step research workflows where a single very-good model beats orchestration
Replacing the o1/o3 + 4o mental switch — one model, four effort tiers
Agent products that need both raw IQ and steerability
not_ideal_for: Tight latency / cost budgets — Minimal effort is GPT-4.1-class for 1/23 the cost of High
Specialised coding agent loops — Claude Opus 4.5 still leads on real-repo SWE-bench
Fully offline / open-weights deployments

sources.name: Leon Furze — first impressions of GPT-5

public_view.plain_english:
OpenAI stopped making users pick the right model. GPT-5 is one
endpoint that routes each query to the right compute path — fast
for easy questions, deep reasoning for hard ones. Shipped August
2025, it replaced the GPT-4o / o1 / o3-mini / o4-mini mess that
had accumulated over the previous year. The router is the
interesting technical piece; the raw capability bump is modest.
public_view.analogy:
Apple's product-naming playbook applied to AI. Claude 3.7 also
has a reasoning toggle but makes you flip it; GPT-5 just decides.
Fewer buttons, same output.
public_view.investment_angle:
OpenAI's annualized revenue hit ~$15B right around this launch
— bigger than any other AI-product company at the time. The
consolidation mattered commercially as much as the capability
bump, if not more. Try explaining to a CIO why seat deployments
need three different model SKUs. GPT-5 collapsed that into one
line item. Enterprise seats jumped through Q3–Q4 2025. The 400k
context was in every vendor deck but was probably the third
reason anyone actually switched — after the single surface and
the better reasoning.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Almost every AI-product headline from August 2025 through Q1
2026 traced back to GPT-5 — consumer chat, Copilot-everywhere,
enterprise agents. People still argue whether the capability
jump was a step change or just incremental. That argument
misses the point. The real shift was that OpenAI stopped
shipping seven models and started shipping one.

nodes.body_md:
{/* Dual-language MDX body. English content wrapped in
; Chinese content in
. The global Base.astro CSS rules hide whichever doesn't match html[lang]. Writing zh once the user toggles, tree view stays as-is. Use this pattern when extending to other model pages. */}
## GPT-5 vs late-2025 frontier | Model | Date | Org | Reasoning | Context | Approach | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | Claude 4 Opus | 2025-05 | Anthropic | hybrid (toggle) | 200k | per-request thinking flag | | Gemini 2.5 Pro | 2025-03 | Google | integrated | 1M | always-on thinking budget | | **GPT-5** | **2025-08** | **OpenAI** | **routed** | **400k** | **automatic routing** | | Llama 4 Maverick | 2025-04 | Meta | none | 1M | non-reasoning open | | DeepSeek R2 | 2025-Q3 | DeepSeek | dedicated | 128k | open reasoning | Each frontier lab landed on a slightly different productization choice for "fast vs reasoning": - **OpenAI**: route automatically (one model surface) - **Anthropic**: per-request flag (developer chooses) - **Google**: integrated thinking budget (always reasons, budget controls effort) - **DeepSeek**: separate models (V3 vs R-series) The market hasn't converged — different developers prefer different tradeoffs.
## GPT-5 对比 2025 年末的前沿阵营 | 模型 | 发布 | 厂商 | 推理模式 | 上下文 | 落地方式 | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | Claude 4 Opus | 2025-05 | Anthropic | 混合（可切换） | 200k | 按请求打开推理标志 | | Gemini 2.5 Pro | 2025-03 | Google | 内建 | 1M | 常开推理 + 预算控制 | | **GPT-5** | **2025-08** | **OpenAI** | **自动路由** | **400k** | **路由器自行判断** | | Llama 4 Maverick | 2025-04 | Meta | 无 | 1M | 开源非推理 | | DeepSeek R2 | 2025-Q3 | DeepSeek | 专用推理模型 | 128k | 开源推理 | 前沿各家在「快速 vs 推理」的产品化路线上走出不同选择： - **OpenAI**：自动路由（单一模型入口） - **Anthropic**：按请求标记（让开发者自己决定） - **Google**：内置推理预算（始终推理，靠预算控制投入） - **DeepSeek**：分家两套模型（V3 通用 / R 系推理） 市场还没有收敛——不同开发者偏好不同取舍。

nodes.title: Grok 4 — xAI's Reasoning Frontier

model_spec.architecture: Decoder-only transformer + extended-thinking + Grok 4 Heavy multi-agent ensemble

benchmarks.name: Vision · MMMU-Pro
benchmarks.vs_baseline: Rank #7 of 26 · Top #7 of 26 · Artificial Analysis

feels_like: AA Intelligence Index of 73 at release — leading frontier slot, ahead of o3 (70), Gemini 2.5 Pro (70), Claude 4 Opus (64)
GPQA Diamond all-time high of 88%; Humanity's Last Exam 24%; AIME 2024 94%; MMLU-Pro 87%
Leads Coding Index (LiveCodeBench + SciCode) and Math Index (AIME24 + MATH-500)
256K-token context in API (128K in app); 75 tok/s — slower than o3 but faster than Claude 4 Opus Thinking
Tool-integrated reasoning trained via large-scale RL with verifiable rewards
Launched under controversy — Elon-favoritism concerns and prior Grok-3 benchmark trust issues still weigh on reception
best_for: Math, science, and reasoning research where Grok 4 leads on raw benchmark numbers
Real-time information lookup inside the X (Twitter) product with full timeline access
Tool-using agents that benefit from extended reasoning + verifiable-rewards training
Less-restricted prompting than ChatGPT/Claude defaults
not_ideal_for: Customer-facing products where political-bias risk is a brand concern
Workflows requiring system-prompt-free behaviour — RL tuning shows different defaults depending on scaffold
Self-hosted or air-gapped deployments — closed weights, hosted only via X / API

sources.name: Interconnects — Grok 4 an o3 look-alike in search

public_view.plain_english:
xAI's August 2025 reasoning flagship — Grok 4 and Grok 4 Heavy.
The "Heavy" variant runs multiple reasoning agents in
parallel and synthesizes their outputs, achieving a step
change on the hardest benchmarks (Humanity's Last Exam,
ARC-AGI v2). Released ~6 months after Grok 3, demonstrating
xAI's continued ability to ship at frontier cadence post-
Colossus-cluster build-out.
public_view.analogy:
The "if one model is good, several models reasoning together
is better" thesis at the product level. Heavy is xAI's
answer to OpenAI's o3-pro and Anthropic's
extended-thinking-with-tool-use stacks.
public_view.investment_angle:
Grok 4 Heavy's HLE score (44.4% vs nearest peer ~25%)
forced industry recalibration on "what counts as the
frontier." xAI's $200B valuation discussion in mid-2025
was partly underwritten by Grok 4's benchmark performance
showing the lab could compete on capability, not just on
Elon-distribution. The $300/mo SuperGrok tier (multi-agent
Heavy access) priced at the same level as ChatGPT Pro,
signaling xAI's intent to compete on consumer reasoning
spend, not just X-platform integration.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Grok 4 Heavy sits at the absolute frontier of reasoning
benchmarks in late 2025 / early 2026 — the model that
"what's the hardest thing AI can do today?" debates
cite for the upper bound. Whether the multi-agent
inference architecture is durable or gets matched by
single-model successors (Opus 4.7, GPT-5.5) is the
open competitive question.

nodes.body_md:
## Grok 4 Heavy vs frontier reasoning peers (Q3 2025)

| Model | Date | HLE | ARC-AGI v2 | AIME 2025 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude 4 Opus | 2025-05 | 12% | — | — |
| Gemini 2.5 Pro | 2025-03 | 21.6% | — | ~93% |
| o3 | 2025-04 | 20.3% | ~5% | ~95% |
| GPT-5 | 2025-08 | ~25% | ~10% | ~99% |
| **Grok 4 Heavy** | **2025-08** | **44.4%** | **~16%** | **~95%** |

Grok 4 Heavy's HLE score was the largest single-release benchmark step in 2025, comparable in magnitude to o1's late-2024 reasoning step.

## The multi-agent inference architecture

Grok 4 Heavy spawns multiple parallel reasoning instances ("agents"), each working on the same problem from a different angle. The instances can share intermediate findings during reasoning. A final synthesis step combines outputs.

Compute cost: ~5-20× a single Grok 4 query. The Heavy tier is priced accordingly ($300/mo consumer subscription, premium API tier).

The architecture echoes earlier "self-consistency" work (Wang et al., 2022) that sampled multiple CoT chains and majority-voted — but goes further by allowing inter-agent communication during reasoning, not just at the answer-synthesis stage.

nodes.title: DINOv3 — Meta's Self-Supervised Vision Foundation

model_spec.architecture: Self-supervised ViT + DINO objective with Gram anchoring

feels_like: Vision-language model from Meta AI — see the linked sources below for benchmark and review coverage

See the model spec and sources block for benchmarked use cases
not_ideal_for: Tasks far outside the modalities listed in this model's spec
Workflows where a more recent successor in the same family scores higher

public_view.plain_english:
Meta's August 2025 self-supervised vision encoder — a vision
transformer trained on 1.7 BILLION images without any labels or
captions. The model learns visual structure on its own, then
transfers to detection, segmentation, depth, and feature-matching
tasks. The default backbone for serious computer-vision research
in late 2025.
public_view.analogy:
CLIP learned vision by reading the captions humans wrote about
images — secondhand text knowledge. DINO is the patient observer
who watches a billion images and figures out what matters by
looking, no captions needed.
public_view.investment_angle:
Foundation visual encoders are infrastructure — the moat is
whoever's encoder gets baked into downstream pipelines. DINOv3 is
the de facto vision backbone for academic + open-source CV in
2025-2026, putting Meta in the same upstream position for vision
that Hugging Face has for NLP.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Established that self-supervised pretraining at scale beats
image-text contrastive (CLIP) on most non-zero-shot CV tasks.
Combined with SAM 3 (segmentation) and DINOv3, Meta now anchors
the academic CV stack the way it anchored NLP with Llama.

nodes.body_md:
## What's distinctive

- **No captions required** — pure self-supervised; learns visual structure from raw pixels
- **1.7B image dataset** — largest self-supervised vision pretraining to date
- **Gram anchoring** — novel technique to prevent feature collapse
- **ConvNeXt + ViT variants** — ships as a family, picks architecture per use case

public_view.plain_english:
ByteDance's August 2025 image model — generates 2K-resolution
images in 1.8 seconds via a mixture-of-experts architecture.
Speed is the differentiator: most quality-tier models take 5-15
seconds. Powers ByteDance's Doubao image generation and the
Jimeng creative app, plus is the image backbone for Seedance
video.
public_view.analogy:
Most image models are oil painters — careful, slow. Seedream is
a charcoal-sketch artist who can produce a finished piece in
seconds without losing detail.
public_view.investment_angle:
ByteDance's image+video stack (Seedream + Seedance + Hailuo
counterpart at MiniMax) is the basis of TikTok's coming
generative-video feature pipeline. Seedream's MoE speed makes
creator-tier real-time editing economically viable.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Demonstrated that MoE works for image diffusion as well as
language — same active-parameter trick. Seedream 4.5 / 5.0
refreshes have iterated quickly, and Seedance video is built on
the same architecture.

public_view.plain_english:
Alibaba's open-source video family — Wan 2.2 → 2.5 → 2.6 → 2.7 —
leads the open VBench leaderboard. Wan 2.7 (early 2026) added a
"Thinking Mode" that plans complex multi-shot videos before
generating, similar to Veo 3 Ingredients. Apache 2.0 throughout.
public_view.analogy:
What Qwen is to text, Wan is to video — Alibaba's fully-open
play in a closed-dominated market.
public_view.investment_angle:
Alibaba's open video play, parallel to Qwen's open text play.
Strategic value: anchor Bailian Cloud video offerings and prove
that Alibaba can compete with ByteDance (closed Seedance) on
technical merits without licensing fees.
public_view.why_it_matters:
Currently the strongest fully-open video model on VBench. Pushed
HunyuanVideo and other open competitors to keep up. Made open
video generation a real procurement option for media / agencies.
